rarityNote: Rarity tier per (species,map,method) is derived from the Gen-1 wild encounter slot weights. Grass and Water tables each have 10 fixed slots; the per-slot probability is set by WildMonEncounterSlotChances in data/wild/probabilities.asm: slot0=51/256(19.92%), slot1=51/256(19.92%), slot2=39/256(15.23%), slot3=25/256(9.77%), slot4=25/256(9.77%), slot5=25/256(9.77%), slot6=13/256(5.08%), slot7=13/256(5.08%), slot8=11/256(4.30%), slot9=3/256(1.17%). A species can occupy multiple slots in a table, so its map encounter probability is the SUM of the slot weights it fills, converted to a percent of 256. Tiers: common>=15%, uncommon 5-14%, rare 1-4% (a residual very-rare<1% bucket exists only for a species sitting alone in slot9=1.17%, which does not occur in the Kanto tables). Fishing methods are separate and were NOT tier-scored here because their slots are uniform, not weighted: Old Rod is always Magikarp; Good Rod is a 50/50 pick of Goldeen/Poliwag; Super Rod uses per-map fishing groups where every listed mon is equally likely (1/N). Encounter-rate byte per map (def_grass/water_wildmons N) gates whether ANY wild appears per step but does not change the relative slot tiering. This note describes the method; the catchRateByDex map below is the primary deliverable (the exact catch-rate byte from each species' base_stats .asm, keyed by National Dex number).
